items.name: cookieParser
items.description: Cookie parsing middleware with timing-safe HMAC-SHA256 signature verification, JSON cookie support, secret rotation, and static helper methods. Parses the Cookie header into req.cookies and req.signedCookies. When a secret is provided, signed cookies (s: prefix) are verified using crypto.timingSafeEqual to prevent timing attacks. JSON cookies (j: prefix) are automatically parsed into objects. Exposes req.secret and req.secrets for downstream middleware like res.cookie({ signed: true }) and csrf().

options.notes: Signing secret. Pass an array for key rotation — newest first. When set, populates req.secret and req.secrets.
URI-decode cookie values.

tips: Always use signed cookies for session tokens and sensitive data.
For key rotation, pass secrets as an array: ['new-key', 'old-key']. Cookies are verified against all secrets but signed with the first.
Signature verification uses crypto.timingSafeEqual() to prevent timing attacks — never downgrade to string comparison.
JSON cookies (j: prefix) are auto-parsed — no need to manually JSON.parse cookie values.
res.cookie() with signed: true requires cookieParser to be configured with a secret. It reads req.secret automatically.
The partitioned attribute (CHIPS) isolates cookies per top-level site — useful for third-party embedded widgets.

methods.description: Sign a value with HMAC-SHA256. Returns 's:<value>.<signature>'.
Verify and unsign a signed cookie. Tries all provided secrets (rotation support). Returns the original value or false.

methodReturns.description: The original value if the signature is valid, or false.

methodParams.notes: Secret or array of secrets to try (enables rotation).

methods.description: Serialize a value as a JSON cookie string: 'j:' + JSON.stringify(val).
Parse a JSON cookie string (j: prefix). Returns parsed value or original string if not a valid JSON cookie.

methodReturns.description: Parsed value if string starts with 'j:', otherwise the original string.

methodParams.notes: JSON cookie string to parse. Must start with 'j:' to be treated as JSON.

items.name: csrf
items.description: Double-submit cookie CSRF protection. Generates a cryptographically random token, stores it in a cookie, and validates it on state-changing requests. GET, HEAD, and OPTIONS are automatically skipped. Tokens can be sent via header, body (_csrf field), or query parameter.

options.notes: Cookie name for the CSRF token.
Request header to check for the token.
Token salt length in bytes.
Secret for token generation. Auto-generated if not provided.
HTTP methods that skip CSRF validation.
URL paths to skip (e.g. webhook endpoints).
Custom error handler: (err, req, res, next) => {}.

tips: cookieParser() must be registered before csrf() even without a signing secret.
Use ignorePaths for webhook endpoints that receive external POST requests.
The token is available on req.csrfToken after the middleware runs.

items.name: validate
items.description: Request validation middleware with 11 types and auto-coercion. Validates req.body, req.query, and req.params against a declarative schema. Unknown fields are stripped by default. Returns 422 with structured error messages on failure.

options.notes: Validation rules for `req.body` fields.
Validation rules for `req.query` fields.
Validation rules for `req.params` fields.
Remove fields not defined in the schema.
Custom error handler. Default sends 422 JSON.
Rule: Value type with auto-coercion. One of: `string`, `integer`, `number`, `float`, `boolean`, `array`, `json`, `date`, `uuid`, `email`, `url`.
Rule: Field must be present in the request.
Rule: Default value (or factory function) when the field is missing.
Rule: Minimum string length.
Rule: Maximum string length.
Rule: Minimum numeric value.
Rule: Maximum numeric value.
Rule: Regex pattern the value must match.
Rule: Allowed values whitelist.
Rule: Minimum array length (for `type: 'array'`).
Rule: Maximum array length (for `type: 'array'`).
Rule: Custom validation function. Return a string to indicate an error.

tips: Supported types: string, integer, number, float, boolean, array, json, date, uuid, email, url.
stripUnknown: true (default) prevents mass-assignment attacks by removing unknown fields.
Use validate.field() and validate.object() for programmatic validation outside middleware.

methods.description: Validate a single value against a rule. Returns { value, error }.

methodReturns.description: Object with { value: any, error: string | null }.

methodParams.notes: Rule object, e.g. `{ type: "string", minLength: 2, maxLength: 50, required: true }`.
Field name used in error messages.

methods.description: Validate an object against a schema. Returns { sanitized, errors }.

methodReturns.description: Object with { sanitized: object, errors: string[] }.

methodParams.notes: Schema where each key maps to a rule object for the corresponding field.
Options object. `{ stripUnknown: boolean }` — defaults to true.
